commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Xiaomi `mimo-v2.5-tts-voicedesign` support by listing the model, omitting `audio.voice` only for that model, adding focused provider tests, and documenting the Xiaomi/TTS contract.

PR surface: Source +29, Tests +112, Docs +28. Total +169 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for a source-level reproduction: current main and `v2026.5.28` do not list the voicedesign model and always send `audio.voice` in Xiaomi TTS requests. I did not run a live Xiaomi API call in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair remains; the PR is ready for maintainer review with CI or focused Testbox validation as the main remaining gate.

target.title: fix(xiaomi): support MiMo voicedesign TTS
target.body:
## Summary

- add Xiaomi MiMo `mimo-v2.5-tts-voicedesign` to the speech provider model list
- omit preset `audio.voice` for voicedesign requests while preserving existing preset-voice model behavior
- add tests for configured/default voicedesign style prompts and voice-note transcoding
- document the voicedesign model contract and remove the contributor-side changelog edit

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node --check extensions/xiaomi/speech-provider.ts`
- local patched-provider proof script using the current `extensions/xiaomi/speech-provider.ts` source and a stubbed successful Xiaomi HTTP response
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` *(previous commit, before docs follow-up)*
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/xiaomi/speech-provider.test.ts` *(blocked locally: missing `@vitest/mocker` in `node_modules`; `pnpm install` was attempted once and stalled on repeated npm registry fetch/attestation retries)*

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Xiaomi MiMo voicedesign TTS requests no longer send `audio.voice`, which Xiaomi rejects for `mimo-v2.5-tts-voicedesign`.

Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout on branch `codex/xiaomi-voicedesign-tts`, running the patched `extensions/xiaomi/speech-provider.ts` source through a temporary proof harness. The harness used OpenClaw's Xiaomi provider `synthesize(...)` path and stubbed only the HTTP success response so the emitted provider request body could be inspected without printing secrets.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node /tmp/openclaw-xiaomi-voicedesign-request-proof.mjs`, where the temporary script transpiled the current `extensions/xiaomi/speech-provider.ts`, called `buildXiaomiSpeechProvider().synthesize(...)` with `model: "mimo-v2.5-tts-voicedesign"`, `format: "wav"`, `style: "Warm, natural female voice with clear pronunciation."`, and an intentionally configured `voice: "Chloe"`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "providerId": "xiaomi",
  "modelListed": true,
  "defaultModel": "mimo-v2.5-tts",
  "requestUrl": "https://api.xiaomimimo.com/v1/chat/completions",
  "requestModel": "mimo-v2.5-tts-voicedesign",
  "requestRoles": ["user", "assistant"],
  "firstMessageIncludesStyle": true,
  "audio": { "format": "wav" },
  "hasAudioVoice": false,
  "outputFormat": "wav",
  "fileExtension": ".wav",
  "audioBytes": 17
}
```

Observed result after fix: The patched OpenClaw Xiaomi speech provider lists `mimo-v2.5-tts-voicedesign`, sends the style prompt as the first `user` message, sends the TTS text as the `assistant` message, and emits `audio: { "format": "wav" }` with no `audio.voice` even when a voice is configured.

What was not tested: A live Xiaomi audio response from this local checkout was not completed because this machine has no local `XIAOMI_API_KEY` or `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`, and the redacted key available on the Tencent host returned `401 Invalid API Key` when used from the local proof command. The focused Vitest could not run in this checkout because local dependencies are incomplete and `pnpm install` could not fetch from npm registry.
